task.title: Disabling IPv6 on a connection using nmcli

li.p: The system uses NetworkManager to manage network interfaces.

context.p: You can use the nmcli utility to disable the IPv6 protocol on the command line.

step.cmd: Optional: Display the list of network connections:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection show NAME UUID TYPE DEVICE Example 7a7e0151-9c18-4e6f-89ee-65bb2d64d365 ethernet enp1s0 ...

step.cmd: Set the ipv6.method parameter of the connection to disabled:

li.p: Display the IP settings of the device:
li.codeblock:
# ip address show enp1s0
2: enp1s0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000 link/ether 52:54:00:6b:74:be brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff inet 192.0.2.1/24 brd 192.10.2.255 scope global noprefixroute enp1s0 valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
li.p: If no inet6 entry is displayed, IPv6 is disabled on the device.
Verify that the /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/enp1s0/disable_ipv6 file now contains the value 1:
The value 1 means that IPv6 is disabled for the device.
